text.title: django-pytest-factories Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-pytest-factories as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Manually writing @pytest.fixture def user(db): return UserFactory() after registering UserFactory with pytest-factoryboy.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: pytest-factoryboy already created a 'user' fixture. The manual fixture silently overrides it without warning. All tests using 'user' now get the manual fixture, which may have different behavior (wrong scope, no cleanup). Use only one source per fixture name.

failure-mode.description: Factory Boy + Faker unique values drift: tests asserting that response.data["email"] equals "user@example.com" fail when Faker generates a different email.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Faker generates random data on every run. Never assert on the literal value Faker will produce. Instead, assert on the factory instance's own attribute: assert response.data["email"] == user.email. Always derive expected values from the object, not from the Faker call.

failure-mode.description: bulk_create skipping signals: factories using bulk_create don't trigger post_save, so tests depending on signals see no side effect.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Django's bulk_create bypasses the model save method and all signals. Tests that expect a Celery task to be enqueued or an audit record to be created via post_save will silently not see it when bulk_create is used. Use individual save() or the factory's create() method when signals matter.

failure-mode.description: skip_postgeneration_save missing for Django 4.2+ factories with @factory.post_generation methods.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without skip_postgeneration_save=True in Meta, Factory Boy calls save() after every post_generation hook, causing redundant DB writes. Add skip_postgeneration_save=True and manually save inside the post_generation function only when needed.
